mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in FEDFUNDS transmits an easier discount-rate regime to equities, raising present values and supporting positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The effect is strongest when CPIAUCSL is not accelerating, because stable inflation reduces the risk that policy easing is offset by renewed rate expectations.
regime_hint: monetary easing cycle with stable inflation
failure_condition: Reject if 20d SPX returns are negative or below baseline in periods where FEDFUNDS is falling month-over-month and CPIAUCSL is not accelerating, or if rolling Rank IC stays below 0.03 for any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in UNRATE above recent levels signals weakening labor income and lower aggregate demand, which should pressure earnings expectations and produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The signal fails when unemployment rises only alongside strong payroll growth, because that pattern reflects noisy labor rebalancing rather than true demand deterioration.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered instances of rising UNRATE deliver positive 20d SPX returns in more than 60% of cases, or if the signal loses directional accuracy when PAYEMS is also expanding.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in INDPRO captures weakening industrial output and margin-sensitive demand for cyclicals, which should translate into negative S&P 500 performance over the following month. The mechanism is falsified if output falls but equity returns remain persistently positive, implying that markets are ignoring real-activity deterioration.
failure_condition: Reject if month-over-month declines in INDPRO are followed by positive 20d SPX returns in a majority of occurrences, or if the rolling 3-year hit rate is not below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: When RSAFS rises while HOUST also rises, household and retail demand is broadening rather than just rotating, supporting earnings breadth and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days. The interaction matters because retail sales alone or housing alone can reflect price effects, but both together indicate real demand expansion.
failure_condition: Reject if simultaneous RSAFS and HOUST increases fail to produce above-baseline 60d SPX returns, or if the combined signal adds no predictive value beyond either series alone in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: A FEDFUNDS level above 4.5% signals restrictive policy that compresses valuation multiples and slows risk-taking, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days. The signal is strongest when CPIAUCSL is still elevated, because inflation persistence raises the odds that tight policy remains in place.
failure_condition: Reject if FEDFUNDS above 4.5% is not followed by negative 5d SPX returns more often than not, or if elevated inflation does not improve the signal versus FEDFUNDS alone.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating GDPC1 combined with rising RSAFS indicates synchronized output and spending growth, which should support earnings revisions and positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter. The effect fails when GDP grows but retail sales do not, because output growth without demand confirmation is unlikely to sustain equities.
failure_condition: Reject if synchronized GDPC1 and RSAFS growth does not improve 60d SPX return rates versus single-series benchmarks, or if positive returns do not dominate in the top-growth joint state.

mechanism_narrative: A fall in PAYEMS when UNRATE is already low signals cooling labor demand from a high-employment base, which should weaken forward earnings expectations and generate negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days. The mechanism is invalid if payroll declines occur alongside falling unemployment, because that pattern reflects measurement noise rather than labor-market deterioration.
failure_condition: Reject if consecutive payroll declines do not precede negative 20d SPX returns more than 55% of the time, or if the effect disappears when UNRATE is not simultaneously rising.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock recovery, improving INDPRO together with higher HOUST indicates active real-side normalization in production and construction, which should lift cyclical sentiment and support positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days. The signal fails if output improves without housing recovery, because manufacturing-only rebounds can be too narrow to sustain market breadth.
failure_condition: Reject if joint improvements in INDPRO and HOUST do not produce positive 60d SPX returns more often than baseline, or if the signal is not superior to INDPRO alone in walk-forward evaluation.
